title: tasks-mux gaps: priorities, dependencies, search, status history, bulk ops
summary: Implement the narrowed task-management primitives needed before tasks-mux can serve as the unified routing hub: canonical priority, dependsOn, searchBreakpoints, status history, bulk operations, state-machine validation, and audit logging.
acceptanceCriteria: Breakpoints expose canonical priority: low | medium | high | critical with compatibility behavior for existing context.urgency data.
Breakpoints expose dependsOn: string[] and dependency blocking prevents resolution or approval until prerequisites are complete.
The backend contract exposes searchBreakpoints(query) with filters for status, assignee/responder, priority, free text, projectId, and repoId.
Breakpoint state transitions append status history/timeline entries with timestamps.
Bulk approve, bulk close, and bulk reassign operations provide per-breakpoint success/failure results and preserve partial failure evidence.
Invalid status transitions are rejected consistently by direct operations and bulk operations.
Audit log entries record who changed what and when, including before/after values where available.
git-native, server, GitHub Issues, client, CLI, and MCP surfaces either support the new contract or fail with explicit backend capability errors covered by tests.
Existing breakpoint files, proven-answer behavior, ask/answer/status/poll/claim commands, and responder routing metadata remain backward-compatible.
nonGoals: Do not implement broader #596 items unless needed as extension hooks: notifications, SLA metrics, discussion threads, escalation chains, interactive forms, data export, or backup.
Do not wire SDK or agent-platform routing through tasks-mux in this issue; #634 only makes tasks-mux ready for that routing work.
Do not replace git-native persistence with a database.

plannedPhases.name: Live context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #634, duplicate parent #596, related issues, PR probe, design docs, current tasks-mux code, and git status at runtime.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Run the mandatory REVIEW BEFORE PROCEEDING scan for existing migrations, routes, environment variables, dependencies, imports, tests, and tasks-mux seams.

plannedPhases.name: Scope classification
plannedPhases.purpose: Treat #634 as a narrowed duplicate unless live comments say otherwise, and separate #634 deliverables from broader #596 non-goals.

plannedPhases.name: Architecture contract
plannedPhases.purpose: Design additive schema, backend, state-machine, dependency, audit/history, CLI/MCP/client, backend-parity, and compatibility contracts.

plannedPhases.name: Red-first tests
plannedPhases.purpose: Author acceptance and compatibility tests before source changes, then prove targeted red failures.

plannedPhases.name: Implementation slices
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement shared contract, git-native behavior, server/GitHub/client parity, and CLI/MCP/docs with slice gates.

plannedPhases.name: Verification and review
plannedPhases.purpose: Run deterministic verification commands and maintainer-grade review until quality target is met.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Compare live spec bytes to final artifacts and produce a per-deliverable traceability matrix.

breakpoints.when: Issue #634's duplicate/closed state conflicts with implementation scope.
Final acceptance finds an unresolved compatibility, backend parity, or status-model decision.

qualityGates: Reuse audit begins with the exact required REVIEW BEFORE PROCEEDING marker.
Architecture records runtimeCallPaths and affectedFiles before implementation.
Red tests are added before production source changes and fail for expected missing-feature reasons.
Priority and dependsOn are canonical schema fields and legacy breakpoint fixtures still parse.
Dependency blocking is enforced across answer, approve, complete, and bulk approval paths.
searchBreakpoints supports combined status, assignee/responder, priority, text, projectId, and repoId filters or returns explicit unsupported capability errors.
Bulk operations return per-id results, record audit entries, and reject invalid transitions without corrupting unrelated breakpoints.
Status history records every transition with timestamp, actor/source metadata where available, and before/after values where relevant.
CLI, MCP, and client surfaces expose search and bulk operations with tests.
Server, GitHub, and external tracker-adjacent mappings preserve hidden payload compatibility and explicit unsupported behavior.
Existing ask, answer, status, poll, claim, responder routing, and proven answer behavior remain covered.
Final review score is at least 90 with no blocking findings.
finalArtifactCommand:
set -euo pipefail
git status --short --branch
git diff --stat
git diff -- . ':!node_modules'
